page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores using Intel Xeon 8581C processors running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1488.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.75 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 165,331.17 and a Passmark CPU score of 115,628.08. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 12,790.33 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 246.84 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), enables strong performance in cryptography, vector calculations, and matrix operations.

The instance is highly capable for memory-intensive and compute-heavy workloads. It excels in database operations, showing a Redis SET performance of 209.65 million operations per second, and handles static web serving throughput at 215.6 GB/sec. Additionally, the platform supports large-scale software compilation and LLM inference, processing 70B model prompts at 38.14 tokens per second. This profile makes it suitable for enterprise databases, large-scale data processing, and memory-bound simulation workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for resource-intensive workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 192 dedicated vCPUs from 96 physical Intel Xeon 8581C cores running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 1488.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results highlight its exceptional capability in database operations, achieving 209.65 million Redis SET operations per second, and high-throughput web serving. This server is optimized for large-scale databases, memory-bound enterprise applications, and multi-threaded computational workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is a memory-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1488 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon 8581C processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is an x86_64 server with 192 vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise databases and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 1488 GB of RAM on Intel Xeon 8581C processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and LLM inference
